core_meaning: Emphasizing the subject or object with 'même' (even/self)
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing personal agency through reflexive forms
when_to_use: When emphasizing that someone does something themselves
essential_vocabulary_slots: même, moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, ils, elles
common_learner_mistakes: Placing même after instead of before the word to emphasize
essential_vocabulary: même, moi, toi, lui, elle, nous, vous, ils, elles, soi
why_it_matters: Essential for emphasizing personal agency and responsibility

core_meaning: Emphasizing a specific element by restructuring the sentence
primary_communicative_fn: Focusing emphasis on a particular sentence element
when_to_use: When highlighting who did something or when something happens
pattern: c'est + élément + qui/que + reste de la phrase
essential_vocabulary_slots: c'est, qui, que, moi, elle, demain, nous, partons
chunk_compatibility: Natural in focused statements and clarifications
key_forms: C'est moi qui, C'est elle que, C'est demain que
essential_vocabulary: c'est, qui, que, moi, elle, demain, nous, partons, fait, vu

core_meaning: Moving an element to the front of the sentence for emphasis
primary_communicative_fn: Creating stylistic emphasis through word order
when_to_use: In formal writing and literary contexts for sophisticated emphasis
key_variations: Des problèmes, il y en a beaucoup
essential_vocabulary_slots: livre, je, lu, Paris, rencontré, Marie, problèmes, en
common_learner_mistakes: Forgetting the comma that signals fronting
Not adjusting pronoun position when fronting objects
chunk_compatibility: Used in narrative and emphatic contexts
key_forms: Ce livre, je l'ai lu; À Paris, j'ai rencontré
essential_vocabulary: livre, je, lu, Paris, rencontré, Marie, problèmes, en, comma

core_meaning: Expressing that something is precisely what matters
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing the core point or solution
when_to_use: When stating what really matters or what must be done
key_variations: Ce que je veux, c'est de la paix
Ce qu'il faut faire, c'est attendre
essential_vocabulary_slots: ce que, je veux, c'est, paix, faut, faire, attendre
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing pseudo-cleft with regular c'est... qui/que
chunk_compatibility: Natural in argumentative and persuasive contexts
key_forms: Ce que je veux, c'est; Ce qu'il faut, c'est
essential_vocabulary: ce que, veux, c'est, paix, faut, faire, attendre, vrai
why_it_matters: Essential for assertive emphasis in French

core_meaning: Detaching a phrase from its normal position and resuming with a pronoun
primary_communicative_fn: Emphasizing a topic through syntactic separation
when_to_use: In conversational French when emphasizing a topic
key_variations: Ma sœur, elle habite à Lyon
Ce problème, je ne le comprends pas
essential_vocabulary_slots: sœur, elle, habite, Lyon, problème, je, le, comprends
common_learner_mistakes: Not using the corresponding pronoun to resume
Confusing with fronting (no comma pause in fronting)
key_forms: Ma sœur, elle habite; Ce problème, je ne le comprends
essential_vocabulary: sœur, elle, habite, Lyon, problème, je, le, comprends, pas
why_it_matters: Essential for conversational French emphasis patterns

core_meaning: Adding emphasis by repeating the subject/object at the end
when_to_use: In informal and conversational contexts for affective emphasis
essential_vocabulary_slots: il, est, gentil, garçon, je, l'aime, bien, voiture
common_learner_mistakes: Using right dislocation in formal writing
chunk_compatibility: Natural in informal and affective speech
key_forms: Il est gentil, ce garçon; Je l'aime bien, ma voiture
essential_vocabulary: il, est, gentil, garçon, je, l'aime, bien, voiture, très
why_it_matters: Essential for affective and informal emphasis

structure_label: Emphasis with « à + nom/pronoun + de + infinitif »
core_meaning: Emphasizing who performs an action or whose responsibility it is
when_to_use: When emphasizing whose turn or responsibility it is
essential_vocabulary_slots: à, moi, toi, lui, elle, de, décider, jouer, corriger, professeur
chunk_compatibility: Natural in assigning turns and responsibilities
key_forms: À moi de, À vous de, C'est au professeur de
essential_vocabulary: à, moi, toi, lui, elle, de, décider, jouer, corriger, professeur
why_it_matters: Essential for assigning turns and responsibilities

core_meaning: Emphasizing the subject or object with tonic pronouns in addition to verb conjugation
when_to_use: In conversational French when emphasizing the subject
essential_vocabulary_slots: moi, je, lui, elle, la, connais, bien, pense, sait
common_learner_mistakes: Omitting the tonic pronoun in speech
Placing tonic pronoun in wrong position
key_forms: Moi, je pense; Lui, il sait; Elle, je la connais
essential_vocabulary: moi, je, lui, elle, la, connais, bien, pense, sait, pas

core_meaning: Restricting meaning to only one element (means 'only')
primary_communicative_fn: Restricting focus to a single element
when_to_use: When emphasizing that only one thing is true or applies
essential_vocabulary_slots: ne, que, vingt, euros, fait, regarder, connais, elle
key_forms: Je n'ai que, Il ne fait que, Je ne connais que
essential_vocabulary: ne, que, vingt, euros, fait, regarder, connais, elle, only
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing limitations and restrictions

core_meaning: Moving time or manner adverbs to the front for emphasis
when_to_use: When emphasizing when or how something happens
essential_vocabulary_slots: hier, maintenant, vite, vu, Paul, comprends, faut, partir
common_learner_mistakes: Using declarative word order when emphasis is intended
key_forms: Hier, j'ai vu; Maintenant, je comprends; Vite, il faut
essential_vocabulary: hier, maintenant, vite, vu, Paul, comprends, faut, partir, comma
why_it_matters: Essential for temporal and manner emphasis

primary_communicative_fn: Intensifying adjectives and verbs for emphasis
when_to_use: When emphasizing strength of feeling or quality
essential_vocabulary_slots: bien, gentil, aime, vrai, est, c'est
key_forms: bien gentil, bien vrai, l'aime bien
essential_vocabulary: bien, gentil, aime, vrai, est, c'est, very, indeed
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing strong feelings and emphasis

when_to_use: When providing contrasting explanation or justification
key_variations: Je veux venir, c'est que je n'ai pas le temps
Il est mean, c'est qu'il est fatigué
essential_vocabulary_slots: c'est que, veux, venir, n'ai pas, temps, est, fatigué
common_learner_mistakes: Using as simple connector instead of true emphasis
essential_vocabulary: c'est que, veux, venir, n'ai pas, temps, est, fatigué, parce que
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing subtle contrasts and justifications

when_to_use: When stressing that everyone or everything is included
essential_vocabulary_slots: tout, monde, est, là, seul, fait, problème
chunk_compatibility: Natural in universal and totality statements
key_forms: Tout le monde, tout seul, tout le problème
essential_vocabulary: tout, monde, est, là, seul, fait, problème, everyone, all
why_it_matters: Essential for expressing universality and completeness

core_meaning: Emphasizing the element that follows as the topic
primary_communicative_fn: Shifting focus to a new topic
when_to_use: When changing focus or introducing a topic for discussion
key_variations: Quant à moi, je pense que...
Quant au problème, il est résolu
essential_vocabulary_slots: quant à, moi, pense, problème, est, résolu
primary_function: Marking topic shifts with quant à
key_forms: Quant à moi, Quant au problème, Quant à elle
essential_vocabulary: quant à, moi, pense, problème, est, résolu, as for, concerning

core_meaning: Using a question form to emphasize a statement
when_to_use: In conversational French for emphatic agreement
essential_vocabulary_slots: est-ce que, n'est-ce pas, important, froid, va, d'accord
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing tag question with actual question
essential_vocabulary: est-ce que, n'est-ce pas, important, froid, va, d'accord, isn't it
why_it_matters: Essential for conversational flow and emphasis
